story.iWant: to preview my generated slide in a web browser
story.soThat: I can see exactly how it looks before using it

task.name: Add Preview Offer to Build Completion (Phase 4)
task.subtask: 1.1: Update .slide-builder/workflows/build-one/instructions.md to add preview offer in Phase 4
1.2: After slide save, display clear file path message: "Slide saved to: .slide-builder/single/slide.html"
1.3: Add prompt: "Open in browser? (y/n)"
1.4: Document the path format for easy copy-paste

task.name: Implement Browser Open Functionality
task.subtask: 2.1: If user accepts, use `open` command (macOS) / `xdg-open` (Linux) / `start` (Windows) to launch default browser
2.2: Pass the absolute file path to the browser
2.3: Handle cross-platform command differences
2.4: Provide fallback message if open fails

task.name: Ensure Slide Dimensions Are Correct
task.subtask: 3.1: Verify viewport meta tag in slide HTML: width=1920, height=1080
3.2: Ensure body/slide container has explicit dimensions: width: 1920px; height: 1080px;
3.3: Add overflow: hidden to prevent scrollbars
3.4: Add viewport auto-scaling CSS for smaller screens

task.name: Validate Self-Contained Slide Structure
task.subtask: 4.1: Ensure all CSS is embedded in style tags (no external stylesheets except fonts)
4.2: Ensure all JavaScript is embedded in script tags (no external scripts)
4.3: Verify Google Fonts loaded via link tag with preconnect
4.4: Verify no other external resources referenced
4.5: Add comment in HTML documenting allowed external dependencies

task.name: Verify Contenteditable Functionality
task.subtask: 5.1: Verify all text elements have contenteditable="true"
5.2: Verify all contenteditable elements have data-field attributes
5.3-5.5: Test text selection and editing in Chrome, Firefox, Safari
5.6: Verify cursor appears on click

task.name: Cross-Browser Testing
task.subtask: 6.1-6.3: Open slide in Chrome, Firefox, Safari - verify identical rendering
6.4: Check for CSS property compatibility issues
6.5: Document any browser-specific workarounds needed
6.6: Run Frontend Test Gate checklist

task.name: Update Status and Complete
task.subtask: 7.1: After successful preview offer, update status.yaml if not already updated
7.2: Log "Slide preview offered" in history
7.3: Display completion message

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given a slide has been built, when the build completes, then the system provides the file path and offers to open in browser
Given the user opens the slide HTML file, when viewed in Chrome, Firefox, or Safari, then the slide renders correctly at 1920x1080
All elements display as designed with no layout issues
Text is editable via click (contenteditable works)
Slides are self-contained (no external dependencies except Google Fonts)

docs.doc: Contains AC definitions, Preview Handler module spec, Phase 4 workflow details, NFRs
HTML Slide Pattern, ADR-003 (self-contained HTML slides), contenteditable requirements

file.description: MODIFY - Contains Phase 4 workflow that needs preview offer enhancement
file.currentState: Phase 4 exists with basic state update and completion message. Step 4 includes "Open in browser? (y/n)" ask block. Currently provides instruction: "Open .slide-builder/single/slide.html in Chrome or Firefox"
file.modification: Enhance Phase 4 to: 1. Display clear file path after save 2. Use platform-specific browser open command (open/xdg-open/start) 3. Handle command failure with fallback message
file.keySection: Phase 4: State Update and Completion - Updates status.yaml with built_count and last_action - Displays completion output with file path - Asks "Open in browser? (y/n)" - Currently just provides instruction to open manually

file.description: Reference template showing correct HTML structure
file.pattern: - viewport meta: width=1920, height=1080 - CSS variables in :root - .slide-wrapper with transform-origin for scaling - .slide container at 1920x1080 - scaleSlide() JavaScript for viewport auto-scaling - Google Fonts preconnect and link

file.description: OUTPUT - Generated slide to be previewed (does not exist until build-one runs)
Status tracking - updated in Phase 4 with preview action
Theme configuration - referenced for verification that slide uses theme correctly

dependencies.dependency: Story 3.2 or 3.3 must be complete (build-one workflow functional)
Google Fonts CDN (only allowed external dependency)

constraints.constraint: Slide preview renders immediately in browser (no loading delay)
Must work in Chrome, Firefox, Safari
Slides are self-contained HTML files - no external CSS or JS except Google Fonts
All text elements MUST have contenteditable="true" and unique data-field attributes
Slides MUST be exactly 1920x1080 pixels with viewport meta tag
Use platform-appropriate command: open (macOS), xdg-open (Linux), start (Windows) Current environment is macOS (darwin)

interface.name: Slide HTML Structure
interface.element: All text with contenteditable="true" and data-field

interface.name: Status Update
interface.field: history: append with action and timestamp

standards.standard: Functional test for contenteditable text editing
Dependency audit - no external resources except Google Fonts
Console error check - no JavaScript errors

locations.location: Frontend Test Gate 3-4-TG1 in story file
Manual browser testing (no automated tests for this story)

ideas.idea: Test scaleSlide() by resizing browser window
Verify contenteditable cursor appears on first click
Check localStorage persistence of edits after refresh
Test with slow network to verify no external dependencies block rendering
Verify file:// protocol works (local file access)

implementationGuidance.approach: This story primarily modifies the existing build-one workflow Phase 4. No new files need to be created - only enhancement of existing functionality.

keyChanges.change: 1. After the existing "Slide saved to..." output, ensure absolute path is shown 2. Change the "Open in browser?" response handling from instruction to action 3. Add platform detection and browser open command execution 4. Add error handling with fallback message

implementationGuidance.codePattern: <check if="user says yes"> <action>Detect platform from environment (darwin = macOS)</action> <check if="platform is darwin (macOS)"> <action>Execute: open [absolute_path_to_slide.html]</action> </check> <check if="platform is linux"> <action>Execute: xdg-open [absolute_path_to_slide.html]</action> </check> <check if="platform is windows"> <action>Execute: start [absolute_path_to_slide.html]</action> </check> <check if="command fails"> <output>Could not open browser automatically. Open the file manually at: [absolute_path]</output> </check> </check>

validationChecklist.item: File path shown clearly after build
All text is clickable and editable
No external dependencies besides Google Fonts
